page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m4.large is an x86_64 general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated Intel Xeon physical cores operating at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz, paired with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The processor includes a 33 MB L3 cache and supports instruction sets such as AVX, AVX512F, and AES. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate a divergence between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng single-core CPU and Geekbench single-core scores falling in the average middle 50% range. However, multi-core scaling is limited, as evidenced by poor multi-core CPU performance in stress-ng and Geekbench, both ranking in the bottom 10%. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, with uncached read and write speeds for large blocks measuring 14.71 GB/sec and 13.41 GB/sec respectively, placing memory performance in the bottom 10% to 25% tier.

Given its dedicated CPU allocation but modest overall performance profile, the ecs.u1-c1m4.large represents a low-resource entry point within the ecs.u1 family. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads that require dedicated CPU execution but do not demand high memory throughput or parallel processing power. Suitable workloads include lightweight single-threaded applications, basic text processing, low-traffic static web serving, and small-scale development or testing environments. It is poorly suited for database operations, multi-threaded compilation, or real-time LLM inference.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m4.large is an x86_64 general-purpose instance featuring 2 dedicated Intel Xeon cores running at 2.5 GHz and 8.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, this server lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, utilizing a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Performance benchmarks show average single-core capabilities, but multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics fall into the lower percentiles. Specifically, stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores sit in the middle 50%, whereas multi-core and database performance rank in the bottom 10%. This instance offers a qualitative entry-level cost profile for dedicated resources, making it suitable for lightweight, single-threaded applications, basic web serving, and development environments that do not require high memory throughput or parallel processing.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m4.large is an x86_64 general-purpose instance with 2 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 1 Gbps network bandwidth, suited for lightweight single-threaded workloads.

tagline: A dedicated dual-core x86_64 virtual server with balanced memory for lightweight, single-threaded utility workloads and basic development tasks.

8.0 GB of system memory (4.0 GB per core)
1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Average single-core CPU and text processing performance
Constrained multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth
Best suited for lightweight web serving and development environments
